name: acdc
description: Internal company extension that provides AI agent skills (executable tools + knowledge) and rules (custom instructions) for Business Central (AL) development in VS Code chat/agent mode.

languageModelTools.name: acdc_get_coding_standard
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the official AL / Business Central coding standard from The Framework for a given topic. Available topics: code-style, naming-conventions, performance, error-handling, events, testing, upgrade, guidelines-rules. Use this tool whenever the user asks how something should be done according to company standards, or before generating or reviewing AL code that must follow company conventions. Returns the full Markdown rule file. If the requested topic is unknown, the tool returns the list of available topics.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Look up an AL / Business Central coding standard from The Framework by topic.

topic.description: The coding standard topic to look up, e.g. 'naming-conventions', 'code-style', 'performance', 'error-handling', 'events', 'testing', 'upgrade', or 'guidelines-rules'.

languageModelTools.name: acdc_list_agent_placeholders
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current mapping of agent/tool placeholder names (e.g. reviewAgent, architectAgent) to their configured concrete values for this workspace. Call this to discover which agents are wired to specific roles, or to resolve a placeholder before mentioning an agent by name.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Show the configured agent placeholder mappings (e.g. reviewAgent → AL Code Review Subagent) for this workspace.

languageModelTools.name: acdc_get_sdd_config
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current Spec-Driven Development configuration for this workspace: the plans folder location, the spec folder naming format, the spec file naming format, and the git branch naming format — plus the reference of every template variable that can appear in those formats (dates, sequences, GUID, USER, HOST, env vars, `{req_name}`, `{slug}`, `{type}`, ...). ALWAYS call this tool BEFORE creating a spec folder, a spec file, or a git branch, so you honour the workspace conventions instead of hardcoding `.github/plans/` or naming shapes.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Show the configured SDD paths and naming formats used by agents.

languageModelTools.name: acdc_list_bcquality_custom_rules
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists the customer/partner-authored BCQuality rules currently installed from configured custom layers (see `acdc.bcquality.customLayers`). Returns each rule's qualified name (in the form `<layerId>__<rule-name>`), its `applyTo` glob, the layer id + repository it came from, and a one-line description. Use this when reviewing AL code so you can decide whether a customer-specific rule applies. Rules are transformed from the fork's `custom/knowledge/*.md` files.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List BCQuality rules imported from configured customer/partner custom layers.

layerId.description: Optional. Filter to a single custom layer id.

languageModelTools.name: acdc_get_bcquality_custom_rule
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the full markdown of a customer/partner-authored BCQuality rule by its qualified name (`<layerId>__<rule-name>`, as returned by `acdc_list_bcquality_custom_rules`). Rule content is served from `globalStorage` and includes the transformed `applyTo` frontmatter plus a provenance banner pointing at the source fork + commit.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read a specific BCQuality custom rule (imported from a customer/partner fork).

languageModelTools.name: acdc_list_bcquality_custom_skills
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Lists the customer/partner-authored BCQuality action skills currently installed from configured custom layers. Returns each skill's qualified name (`<layerId>__<skill-name>`), the layer id + repository it came from, and a one-line description parsed from the SKILL.md frontmatter. Skills come from the fork's `custom/skills/**` folder — either the flat `.md` shape or the agentskills.io folder-per-skill shape.
languageModelTools.userDescription: List BCQuality action skills imported from configured customer/partner custom layers.

layerId.description: Optional. Filter to a single custom layer id.

languageModelTools.name: acdc_get_bcquality_custom_skill
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the full SKILL.md of a customer/partner-authored BCQuality skill by its qualified name (`<layerId>__<skill-name>`, as returned by `acdc_list_bcquality_custom_skills`). Content is served from `globalStorage` and includes a provenance banner pointing at the source fork + commit.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Read a specific BCQuality custom skill (imported from a customer/partner fork).

languageModelTools.name: acdc_render_sdd_path
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Renders the configured spec folder, spec file, and git branch names for a given requirement, substituting workspace-configured templates and the standard template variables (dates, sequences, GUID, USER, HOST, ...). Call this INSTEAD of manually building paths like `.github/plans/{req_name}/{req_name}.spec.md` — the result reflects the user's `acdc.*` SDD settings. Provide `req_name` (kebab-case) and, when creating a spec file, `type` (`spec`, `architecture`, `test-plan`, ...). Optional: `slug`, `seq`, `env`.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render workspace-configured SDD folder, file, and branch paths for a requirement.

slug.description: Optional short slug used by `{slug}`. Defaults to `req_name`.

type.description: Contract type when rendering a spec file: 'spec', 'architecture', 'test-plan', 'plan', 'delivery'.

env.description: Optional environment name (`dev`, `staging`, `prod`) for the `{ENV}` variable.

commands.title: Manage AL Base Code / ISV Code
Sync AL Base Code / ISV Code

acdc.alBaseCode.repositories.markdownDescription: Read-only AL/BC & ISV source repositories mounted into the workspace so agents can read real AL code. **[▶ Open table editor](command:acdc.manageAlBaseCode)**  ·  **[ⓘ Details](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

repository.description: Git repository URL of the AL source (BC base app or ISV product). Leave empty for a manual folder that the extension only mounts and never clones/pulls.

branch.description: Branch to check out (e.g. a localisation/version branch). Ignored for manual folders.

folder.description: Local folder the repository is cloned into, or the folder of a manual source. Leave empty (with a repository set) to use the portable per-user default under %LOCALAPPDATA%\acdc-sources\<repo>.

enabled.description: Whether this source is cloned/pulled and mounted in the workspace.

acdc.alBaseCode.syncOnStartup.markdownDescription: When enabled, keeps the AL source folders up to date on startup: missing folders are cloned (after you approve), and existing folders are pulled to the latest commit of their branch (read-only, never pushed).

enumDescriptions: Mount cloned AL sources as read-only workspace folders (visible in the Explorer).
Expose cloned AL sources through a filesystem MCP server registered in the workspace's .vscode/mcp.json (available to AI agents; no Explorer clutter).
acdc.alBaseCode.accessMode.markdownDescription:
How enabled AL source folders are exposed. `workspace` adds them as read-only workspace folders. `mcp` registers an aggregate filesystem MCP server (`acdc-al-sources`) in the workspace's `.vscode/mcp.json`. Only one mode per workspace — switching triggers a confirm dialog and performs the migration immediately.

**Why workspace scope and not user-profile scope?** VS Code has no stable API exposing the currently active profile to an extension, so the user-profile `mcp.json` cannot be targeted reliably from within an extension. Workspace `.vscode/mcp.json` is unambiguous and always loaded.

acdc.agents.placeholders.markdownDescription: Maps `${placeholderName}` tokens used in agent prose/prompts/skills to concrete agent display names, resolved at runtime. **[▶ Set via quick pick](command:acdc.setAgentPlaceholder)**  ·  **[ⓘ Details](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

additionalProperties.description: Display name of the agent or tool this placeholder resolves to.

acdc.agents.settings.markdownDescription: Per-agent overrides stored in user settings and edited from the AC⚡DC sidebar. Each entry key is an agent id or placeholder key. Use the sidebar for easier editing; raw JSON edits are supported here for power users.

model.description: Preferred language model name for the agent. The sidebar can populate this from the available Copilot models.

argumentHint.description: Argument hint shown when the agent is invoked.

bcReviewSpecialist.description: Override for the review specialist agent used by the workflow view.

disabledTools.description: Tool ids disabled for this agent. The sidebar can toggle full namespaces and individual tools.

handoffs.description: Custom handoff definitions for this agent.

placeholderTarget.description: When the key is a placeholder name, this is the concrete agent it resolves to.

acdc.plansRoot.markdownDescription:
Workspace-relative folder for SDD artifacts (spec, architecture, plan, test-plan, ...). Default: `.github/plans`.

**[▶ Pick folder…](command:acdc.pickSddPlansRoot)**  ·  **[ⓘ Details](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

acdc.specFolderFormat.markdownDescription:
Template for per-requirement folder names under `plansRoot`. Supports date/time, sequence, identity and `{req_name}` / `{slug}` variables.

Examples: `{req_name}`, `SDD-{YYYYMMDD}-{slug}`, `{000seq}-{slug}`.

**[ⓘ Details & full variable reference](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

acdc.specFileFormat.markdownDescription:
Template for spec file names. `{type}` is `spec`, `architecture`, `test-plan`, `plan` or `delivery`.

Examples: `{req_name}.{type}.md`, `{YYYYMMDD}-{slug}.{type}.md`.

**[ⓘ Details & full variable reference](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

acdc.branchFormat.markdownDescription:
Template for git branch names for a spec-driven requirement.

Examples: `feature/{slug}`, `spec/{YYYY}-{MM}-{DD}-{slug}`, `{USER}/{slug}`.

**[ⓘ Details & full variable reference](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

acdc.bcquality.customLayers.markdownDescription:
Customer/partner BCQuality forks whose `custom/knowledge/**` and `custom/skills/**` folders are pulled into this extension's `globalStorage` and made available to agents (rules via `chat.instructionsFilesLocations`, skills via the `acdcListBcqualityCustomSkills` / `acdcGetBcqualityCustomSkill` tools). Files never leave `globalStorage` — nothing is written into your workspace.

Each layer's artifacts are prefixed with `<id>__` to prevent name collisions with the bundled Microsoft/Community layers.

**[▶ Open table editor](command:acdc.manageBcqualityCustomLayers)**  ·  **[▶ Sync now](command:acdc.syncBcqualityCustomLayers)**  ·  **[ⓘ Details](command:acdc.showSettingsHelp)**

id.description: Short layer id used as filename prefix (`<id>__…`). Lowercase, 2–32 chars, letters/digits/hyphens, must start with a letter. Must be unique across entries and must not collide with any bundled agent/skill/instruction name.

name.description: Human-friendly display name (shown in logs and consent prompts).

repository.description: Git URL of the BCQuality fork (public HTTPS or SSH). Only the `custom/` folder is pulled.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or 40-hex commit SHA to pin. Prefer a SHA for reproducibility. Defaults to `main` when empty.

tokenSecretKey.description: Optional. Key used to look up a Personal Access Token from VS Code SecretStorage (never store the token in settings.json). Only needed for private repositories.

enabled.description: Whether this layer is synced and exposed to agents.

acdc.bcquality.syncOnStartup.markdownDescription: When enabled, refresh every enabled BCQuality custom layer on startup: resolve the pinned `ref` on the remote, and re-sync only if the resolved SHA differs from the last install. Read-only — nothing is pushed back to the fork.

acdc.bcquality.registerInstructionsFileL.markdownDescription: When enabled, after a successful sync the extension adds its `globalStorage/bcquality-custom/instructions` folder to your user-scoped `chat.instructionsFilesLocations` setting so Copilot Chat auto-discovers the imported `.instructions.md` files with their `applyTo` globs. First-time addition asks for consent. Turn off if you only want the LM-tool access path (`acdcGetBcqualityCustomRule` / `acdcListBcqualityCustomRules`).

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run regenerate:agents && npm run package
scripts.regenerate:agents: npm run inject:avatar-greeting && npm run inject:avatar-greeting:source && npm run inject:avatar-greeting:overlay && npm run normalize:em-dash
scripts.pipeline:assets: npm run sync:sources && npm run normalize:agents && npm run normalize:em-dash && npm run rename:personas && npm run inject:avatar-greeting && npm run generate:contributions && npm run apply:contributions
